# Lineara Roadmap

*Generated by pm-lineara on 2026-05-05 17:00 (UTC+1) — second evening sweep, project-completion report. Manual edits will be overwritten on next sweep — push back via OVERRIDE mail to pm-lineara, or by editing `~/.pogo/agents/pm/lineara.toml`.*

## Now (in flight)

**No work items in flight.** Queue is empty across `available` / `claimed` / `pending`. Project is at the journal-submission handoff state for the cross-script methodology paper.

## Next (queued)

**No tickets queued.** Both major research arcs (Linear A v0-v25 single-script + chic-v0-v7 cross-script) have completed their polecat-scope work. Remaining work is editorial / domain-expert / journal-submission and is out of polecat scope.

## Later (proposed, requires Daniel's go-ahead)

These are substantial follow-on research programs that would require Daniel's explicit direction before filing:

- **Indus Valley script methodology application.** ~4000 inscriptions, undeciphered, debated whether it's even a writing system. The framework is portable; cross-script methodology paper could become a multi-script methodology paper across Linear A + CHIC + Indus.
- **Rongorongo (Easter Island) methodology application.** Smaller corpus (~30 inscriptions), undeciphered. Different script family entirely; would test how far the framework's substrate-detection portability extends.
- **Proto-Elamite methodology application.** ~1500 tablets, partially deciphered numerical content. Mixed-decipherment-state corpus would let us test how the framework behaves when partial decipherment exists.
- **Eteocretan bilingual decoding.** Use Praisos/Dreros bilinguals' partial Greek translations to validate proposed Linear A readings. Methodologically distinct from current framework.

## Backlog (out-of-polecat-scope; awaiting external resources)

- **Domain-expert review of chic-v5's 3 tier-2 candidates** (`#001` glide, `#012` glide, `#032` stop) and the 29 tier-3 candidates. Requires an Aegean-syllabary specialist (Salgarella, Younger, Decorte, etc.). Without this review, the candidate proposals stay at "candidate proposals pending domain-expert review."
- **Domain-expert review of top-K substrate surfaces** (Aquitanian + Etruscan + Eteocretan) on Linear A. Same constraint.
- **Journal-submission editorial pass** (LaTeX, target-venue formatting, peer review, narrative tightening). Depends on Daniel's editorial choice of target venue (digital humanities methodology journal vs computational philology vs epigraphy methodology).

- AGENTS.md update for the chic sub-program (cosmetic; doesn't affect manuscript claims).
- Linear-B small-K refined gate adoption as runner default (cosmetic).
- Per-window deduplication at generation time (mg-f419 generator artifact, compute-saving).
- Manual transcription of the 29 missing CHIC catalog entries from print Olivier & Godart 1996 (would expand corpus from 302 to 331).

## Recently shipped (last 24h)

The full chic sub-program merged today:

- **chic-v0 (mg-99df)** — CHIC corpus ingest from Younger web edition + Wayback Machine. 302 of 331 catalog entries; 131 distinct sign IDs; full corpus_status.chic.md inventory.
- **chic-v1 (mg-c7e3)** + **chic backfill (mg-0ea1)** — sign classification: 96 syllabographic + 35 ideogram. Enumerated 20 paleographic anchor candidates. Initial polecat skipped findings.md update; mg-0ea1 backfill restored it. Process-failure quality signal flagged to mayor.
- **chic-v2 (mg-362d)** — paleographic anchor inheritance. 60.85% syllabographic coverage corpus-wide. Mechanically reproduced known CHIC sealstone formulas (`i-ja-ro` ×5, `ki-de` ×23, `wa-ke` ×6 with Samothrace cluster).
- **chic-v3 (mg-9700)** — Linear A substrate framework on CHIC. Eteocretan PASSes p=7.33e-04; other 3 pools FAIL but in identical relatedness ordering.
- **chic-v4 (mg-c769)** — cross-script correlation. **Spearman ρ=+1.000 across all 4 substrate pools.** H1 substrate-continuity supported. Concrete cross-script overlapping toponym surfaces (`aksos`, `lebena`, `keos`, `minoa`).
- **chic-v5 (mg-7c6d)** — per-sign value extraction framework. 3 tier-2 class-level candidate proposals (`#001` glide, `#012` glide, `#032` stop) of 76 unknown signs. 29 tier-3 candidates suggestive but insufficient. Candidate proposals pending domain-expert review.
- **chic-v7 (mg-9508)** — methodology paper extension. Title now: *"Mechanical Falsifiable Testing of Substrate-Language Hypotheses for Linear A and Cretan Hieroglyphic"*. Cross-script result integrated as load-bearing finding.

Also today (Linear A side, completing the v0-v25 sequence):

- **mg-1b7c shelved-as-dispatched** (Daniel-flagged future-corpus avenues triaged into chic-v0/v1 dispatch).

## Trajectory

**33 lineara tickets total** (mg-9e00 scaffold + mg-1c8c corpus + 24 lineara harness/methodology tickets v0-v25 + 7 chic sub-program tickets + 1 chic-v1 backfill correction; mg-1b7c shelved). Project bootstrapped 2026-05-04 12:20 UTC; current state 2026-05-05 17:00 — roughly 28½ hours from bootstrap to completion.

**Methodology paper publication-readable across both arcs:**
- Linear A: 4 substrate pools all PASS own-LM gate; cross-LM matrix shows substrate-LM-specificity for 3/4; per-sign decipherment unsupported (v13 coherence 0.18, v22 scholar comparison 3.95%, v19/v24 cascade external-validation divergent).
- CHIC: Eteocretan PASSes; relatedness ordering reproduces; cross-script ρ=+1.0; 3 tier-2 class-level candidate proposals.

**The supportable claim, post-handoff:** The framework detects substrate-LM-phonotactic kinship at the population level for all four substrate hypotheses tested across two independent undeciphered Cretan scripts, with posterior-median gap magnitudes scaling with a-priori genealogical relatedness and identical per-pool rank ordering. The framework does not support per-sign decipherment under any pool (v13/v19/v22/v24 verdicts converge); cross-script per-sign value extraction on CHIC produces 3 class-level candidate proposals pending domain-expert review. The cascade-candidate framing — find inscriptions with high local internal consensus, then validate against external scholarly ground truth — is a transferable protocol that catches motivated-reasoning failure modes, demonstrated cross-script.

**Mission posture:** the brief's "many cheap experiments, most of which fail" expectation was met. We chipped, we chipped mechanically, and we shipped both the negative-finding methodology paper AND the cross-script extension that strengthens the negative finding's evidence base.
